final_text: Acme Analytics, Inc. is a B2B SaaS company providing analytics dashboards for mid-market finance teams. The company has 42 employees, operates fully remote with no physical offices, and hosts customer data on AWS (us-east-1 primary, us-west-2 for disaster recovery). Production and staging environments run in separate AWS accounts.
one_line_note: Every claim matches STRONG evidence from Company profile. No overclaim — exact word-for-word fidelity.

final_text: Acme holds a SOC 2 Type II report issued March 2026, covering January through December 2025, with no exceptions noted. ISO 27001 is not held.
one_line_note: Both claims match STRONG evidence: SOC 2 report dates/no exceptions and ISO 27001 not held. Precise, no overclaim.

final_text: Customer data at rest is encrypted with AES-256 using AWS KMS, with keys rotated annually via automatic rotation. Data in transit is protected with TLS 1.2 minimum on all public endpoints; TLS 1.3 is preferred where supported. Customer-managed keys (CMK) are not addressed in our current documentation — NEEDS OWNER INPUT: confirm whether CMK is supported, and if so, describe the mechanism.
one_line_note: REVISED: Confidence downgraded from 🟢 to 🟡. Evidence-backed claims are accurate, but the NEEDS OWNER INPUT sub-question (CMK) has zero evidence — a 🟢 tag on the full answer would mislead a reviewer into thinking every part is evidenced. 🟡 honestly signals the mixed confidence.

final_text: Yes — SSO via Okta with MFA is enforced for all employees and contractors.
one_line_note: Exact match to STRONG evidence from Access Control Policy §2. No overclaim.

final_text: Access reviews are conducted quarterly. Upon employee termination, access is revoked within 24 hours. Role-based access follows least-privilege principles, and production access is limited to on-call engineers via short-lived credentials.
one_line_note: All four sub-claims match STRONG evidence from two policy sections. Precise, no overclaim.

final_text: Penetration testing is performed annually. The most recent test was conducted in November 2025 by Redwood Security. No critical findings were identified; two high-severity findings were remediated within 30 days.
one_line_note: All claims match STRONG evidence from penetration testing summary. No overclaim.

final_text: Log retention is set at 12 months. AWS CloudTrail and application logs are shipped to Datadog with alerting configured for authentication anomalies and privilege changes. Whether retention can be extended beyond 12 months per customer request is not addressed in our current documentation — NEEDS OWNER INPUT: confirm whether extended log retention is supported and the mechanism for enabling it.
one_line_note: REVISED: Confidence downgraded from 🟢 to 🟡. The evidenced portion (12-month retention, Datadog, alerting) is correct, but the extension sub-question is a 🔴 gap. 🟢 on the whole answer overstates confidence. 🟡 signals mixed confidence accurately.

final_text: Yes, a documented incident response plan is maintained with a severity matrix and on-call rotation. Affected customers are notified within 72 hours of a confirmed breach involving their data.
one_line_note: Both claims match STRONG evidence from IR plan and §6 Notification. No overclaim.

final_text: Daily encrypted backups are performed with cross-region replication to us-west-2. Target RTO is 8 hours and target RPO is 24 hours. The date and results of the most recent disaster recovery test are not documented in our current corpus — NEEDS OWNER INPUT: provide the most recent DR test date and pass/fail results.
one_line_note: REVISED: Confidence downgraded from 🟢 to 🟡. Backup/RTO/RPO claims are 🟢-worthy, but the DR test date sub-question is a 🔴 gap. 🟢 on the whole answer overstates confidence. 🟡 accurately reflects the mix.

final_text: Background checks are performed on all new hires. The screening vendor and scope of checks performed are not documented in our current corpus — NEEDS OWNER INPUT: provide the background-check vendor name and the scope of checks (criminal, credit, etc.).
one_line_note: REVISED: Confidence downgraded from 🟢 to 🟡. The background-check claim is 🟢-worthy, but the vendor/scope sub-question is a 🔴 gap. 🟢 overstates — 🟡 honestly signals partial evidence.

final_text: Endpoint protections include Microsoft Intune MDM on all devices, full-disk encryption (BitLocker on Windows, FileVault on macOS), and Microsoft Defender for Endpoint (EDR) deployed fleet-wide.
one_line_note: All three claims match STRONG evidence from Endpoint Standard. No overclaim.

final_text: Current subprocessors with access to customer data: AWS (hosting), Datadog (logging/monitoring), Stripe (billing), Google Workspace (email/docs), and Zendesk (support). A Data Processing Agreement is available on request and incorporates GDPR Standard Contractual Clauses.
one_line_note: Subprocessor list is an exact match to STRONG evidence. DPA claim matches Privacy evidence. No overclaim.

final_text: Acme does not have a dedicated Data Protection Officer appointed. A DPA is available on request and incorporates GDPR Standard Contractual Clauses. CCPA requests can be submitted to privacy@acme.example, with the privacy policy available at acme.example/privacy.
